meta_title: For Students: API integration guide to Solve can't debug code (Beginner Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to API integration guide and overcome can't debug code. A Beginner-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: API Integration Guide to Fix Debugging Issues
content.seo_description: Master API integration and conquer debugging hurdles. This beginner guide uses Antigravity and Claude Code to accelerate your first real-world SaaS project.
content.answer_block: Debugging API failures requires a shift from manual guessing to systematic observability. By leveraging Claude Code to analyze stack traces and Antigravity to simulate environment variables, you can isolate network latency from logic errors. Follow this framework to bridge the gap between broken code and a production-ready feature.
content.intro:
The screen glows, the cursor blinks, and your console is bleeding red. You have spent hours chasing a cryptic 403 error, convinced your API integration is solid, yet the data refuses to flow. This is the rite of passage for every AI builder—the moment where frustration threatens to stall your momentum.

But here is the secret: debugging isn't about being a genius; it is about having a superior feedback loop. By integrating Claude Code for intelligent code analysis and Antigravity for rapid environment simulation, you transform your development environment from a black box into a transparent, actionable system.

steps.title: Map the API Contract
steps.description: Before writing code, define the expected schema. Debugging starts by knowing exactly what the server expects versus what you are sending.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze this API documentation and generate a TypeScript interface for the request and response bodies.
steps.claude_code_command: claude read ./api-docs.md --task 'Create a schema definition file'

steps.title: Isolate the Request Logic
steps.description: Abstract your API calls into a single service file. This prevents logic errors from bleeding into your UI components.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Refactor this API call into a clean, modular service function with error handling.
steps.claude_code_command: claude edit ./src/services/api.ts --patch 'Implement axios interceptors for error logging'

steps.title: Simulate Environment Variables
steps.description: Hardcoded keys are a debugging nightmare. Use Antigravity to inject mocks securely and test boundary conditions.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a mock environment setup that simulates a failed authentication scenario.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --env-file .env.test --task 'Run integration tests against mock server'

steps.title: Automate Traceback Analysis
steps.description: Stop reading logs manually. Use Claude Code to parse the stack trace and identify the specific line causing the failure.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze the attached stack trace and identify the root cause of the 500 error.

steps.title: Validate with Unit Tests
steps.description: Ensure your fix doesn't break existing functionality by running targeted unit tests against your API service.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a suite of Jest tests to verify the API service handles edge cases correctly.

pro_tips: Always log the full request object before it hits the network; 90% of bugs are malformed headers.
Use a 'Circuit Breaker' pattern in your services to prevent your app from crashing during API downtime.
Never debug in production; use a staging environment that mirrors your production config exactly.
Implement request ID tracking to correlate client-side errors with server-side logs instantly.
If an API returns a 429, implement an exponential backoff strategy instead of retrying immediately.
content.next_steps: Once your API integration is stable, document your process in a GitHub README to showcase your problem-solving skills to future employers.
content.conversion_block: Stop fighting your code and start mastering your environment. You have the tools, the guide, and the path to becoming a top-tier engineer. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
